name: provision_source
description: Register a new data source. For realtime sources backed by the upstream integration provider with a trigger component configured under `adapter_config`, the server auto-deploys the named trigger component pointing at a freshly-minted webhook URL — end-to-end with no upstream-provider UI step. Resolve the `component_id` and required `configured_props` via `list_trigger_components` first. The returned `webhook_signing_secret` is shown ONCE — surface it to the user for their records. Pass `ingest_to_graph=true` to ALSO feed each incoming webhook event into the user's knowledge graph (alongside firing agents). NOTE: graph-collected webhook data is stored UNENCRYPTED — the third-party-tooling collection surface skips DEK by design. Mention this to the user before flipping it on.

provider.description: Provider id. Use the upstream integration provider's id for realtime sources, or 'custom' for manual ingestion.

adapter_config.description: Adapter-specific config. To auto-deploy a trigger via the upstream integration provider, include the trigger component descriptor (`component_id` + `configured_props`) under the provider's adapter-config key — see `list_trigger_components`.

ingest_to_graph.description: Opt the source into knowledge-graph ingestion. When true, every webhook event flowing through this source's URL is enqueued for extraction (plaintext storage). Default false.
